docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:109:- The release artifact `ios/NmpPodcast.app` runs on iPhone 12 hardware (build + install + the 20 scripted scenarios run).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fd8f173.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:22:- D4 one writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:61:✅ D4 (one writer per fact): kernel actor is single-threaded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:442:+### D4 Audit: One Writer Per Fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:611:+  - Reconciler back-pressure: deliberately stall the Swift main thread for 250 ms; assert no actor stall, deltas accumulate and replay correctly when the main thread resumes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:958:   353	  - Reconciler back-pressure: deliberately stall the Swift main thread for 250 ms; assert no actor stall, deltas accumulate and replay correctly when the main thread resumes.
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:13:4. **Buffered batching.** Inbound events for one view are batched into a single `ViewBatch` per actor tick at ≤60Hz; backpressure drops batches in favor of a single `FullState` catch-up. The platform's reactive primitive sees one re-render per tick, not per event.
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:15:**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. The ≤60Hz cap is D8 (reactivity contract per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D8; budget validated by ADR-0002 in `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` §7.2).
docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md:9:All `on_event_inserted` / `on_event_removed` / `on_projection_changed` calls happen **synchronously on the actor thread**, inline with the triggering `CoreMsg`. This preserves the single-writer invariant and the actor's deterministic message ordering.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:42:- S3/S4/S5 use VerifiedEvent + EventStore::insert for event pressure?
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:45:- stress-gate-fast includes S4?
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:50: .../bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs   | 117 ++++----
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:81:+    /// events do.  Enables S3/S4/S5 event-pressure gates without a live relay.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:118:+/// Used by S3/S4/S5 stress scenarios to create real emit pressure without a
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:194:+        // D4: all events are persisted before kind-specific dispatch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:283:-        // D4: route through EventStore (the single writer).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:284:+        // D4: route through EventStore (the single writer) for ALL deliveries,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:653:+//! Shared helpers used across S3/S4/S5 stress scenarios.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:785:-    // The cycles gate verifies the scenario ran without stalling, not that it hit the
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:787:-    // to pass on macOS while still catching severe stall regressions (< 600/sec).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:789:+    // 60% to catch severe stall regressions without a false-fail on host-timer jitter.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1289:diff --git a/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs b/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1291:--- a/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1292:+++ b/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1294: //! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S4
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1295: //! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1297:-//! Simulates 12 × 250 ms main-thread stalls during a 60-s event stream.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1299:-//! 1. Actor command queue does not grow during stall (actor is not blocked).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1300:-//! 2. Listener backlog after each stall is bounded by emit_hz × stall_ms.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1301:-//! 3. On stall release, emits arrive in monotonic rev order.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1304:+//! 250 ms main-thread stalls during a 60-s event stream.  During each stall
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1308:-//! runner per scenarios.md §S4. This Rust host implementation provides
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1310:-//! returning, simulating a slow consumer. The actor must not stall.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1312:+//! 1. Actor is not blocked during stall (configure() returns immediately).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1313:+//! 2. On stall release, emits arrive in monotonic rev order.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1314:+//! 3. Stale-rev detection: emits produced during stall may have lower rev
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1315:+//!    than post-stall emits — counted as stale-rev pairs per stall.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1318: //! D1 (best-effort rendering): on stall release, emit order is monotonic.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1333:-/// Number of 250 ms stalls to inject (spec: 12).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1337:-/// Interval between stalls: 4 s keeps all 12 stalls within the 60 s window.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1338:-/// (12 × 4 s = 48 s; last stall starts at t=44 s, ends at t=44.3 s — well within 60 s.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1339:-/// The spec says "12 stalls × 250 ms over 60 s"; it does not prescribe exact spacing.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1344: extern "C" fn stall_cb(ctx: *mut c_void, payload: *const c_char) {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1347:-    // If stalling, sleep to simulate blocked main thread.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1348:+    // Simulate blocked main thread during stall window.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:1352:@@ -66,24 +60,13 @@ extern "C" fn stall_cb(ctx: *mut c_void, payload: *const c_char) {

Both T29 + T30 heavily active per worktree filesystem activity: T29 has split mem.rs → mem/ + types.rs → types/, modified kernel/ingest.rs + mod.rs + nostr.rs, ran firehose-bench live 3 times (M1 regression check). T30 has modified all 5 planner files + audit test. T31 STILL HELD — it needs T29's VerifiedEvent + EventStore::insert path for the S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection. Once T29 lands, T31 can use the real API instead of waiting for relay. No completions this tick. Disk 95%, no master build issues. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6665:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:60`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:63`, `s5_reentrancy.rs:95`: S3/S4/S5 inject synthetic events into the read cache, not `VerifiedEvent` + `EventStore::insert`. `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:427` writes directly to `self.events`. Fix: create signed/valid events and route through the normal verify/store ingest path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6666:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s1_mount_unmount.rs:117`, `s2_dispatch_flood.rs:154`, `s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:72`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:160`: gates are not enforced per `gates.md`. Examples: S1 uses 60% not 90%; S2 uses 98% not 600k/100%; S3 uses 1k not 100k; S4 stale-rev/apply gates are missing or hardcoded. Fix: gate exact spec metrics or fail as unobservable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6674:- T31 report path is `docs/perf/m10.5/S1/`; `stress-gate-fast` includes S4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6683:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:60`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:63`, `s5_reentrancy.rs:95`: S3/S4/S5 inject synthetic events into the read cache, not `VerifiedEvent` + `EventStore::insert`. `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:427` writes directly to `self.events`. Fix: create signed/valid events and route through the normal verify/store ingest path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6684:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s1_mount_unmount.rs:117`, `s2_dispatch_flood.rs:154`, `s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:72`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:160`: gates are not enforced per `gates.md`. Examples: S1 uses 60% not 90%; S2 uses 98% not 600k/100%; S3 uses 1k not 100k; S4 stale-rev/apply gates are missing or hardcoded. Fix: gate exact spec metrics or fail as unobservable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6692:- T31 report path is `docs/perf/m10.5/S1/`; `stress-gate-fast` includes S4.
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:96:  up the `update_rx` channel and stall every subsequent emit (which is
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:115:| S2 | Dispatch flood | mpsc backpressure | bible #3 (fire-and-forget) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:117:| S4 | Reconciler back-pressure | listener channel growth | bible #9, D1 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:248:| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S4 (reconciler back-pressure) + code proof (ProfileCard.placeholder) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:251:| **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:261:| **Nightly** | daily | All ten, S1 full 10-min, S4 250 ms stalls × 60, S9 100 flap, S10 60s suspend | Mac mini self-hosted runner, iPhone 12 device |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/7d16b3f.md:15:2. **`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:236`** — duplicate cache hits return before `VerifiedEvent::try_from_raw` and `store.insert`, so D4 is still broken for duplicate relay delivery. Provenance never merges in `EventStore`, and invalid duplicate payloads can still bump `relay_count`. Route duplicates through store first, then update the read-cache from the store outcome.
docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:246:iOS impl: `SpeechAnalyzer` + `SpeechTranscriber` (iOS 26 APIs already used in `TranscriptionService.swift`). On-device model download via `AssetInventory.assetInstallationRequest(supporting:)` happens inside the bridge on first `Probe` for the language; emits `ModelDownloading` progress events. Bounded state: one `SpeechAnalyzer` per active transcription token; cleaned up on completion or cancel. Idempotent probe.
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1244:+For installations with O(100k+) watermarks (an edge case — typical apps see O(100)–O(10k)), the in-memory map switches to a lazy-loaded variant that pages on demand. Threshold and switching logic deferred to M4 when the negentropy engine's real-world row count is measured.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1426:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1450:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1112:+- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1976:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1767:+All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1998:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3493:A few issues are now concrete rather than stylistic: the design has at least one hard LOC conflict with the copy-verbatim requirement, and the screenshot gate currently depends on an agent-only UI introspection path. I’m pulling the exact lines for those plus the doctrine/D4 ownership concerns.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2002:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2004:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2108:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2251:236:- com.podcastr.next.Podcastr, devicectl, 00008150-001E118E3CD2401C, Pablo's iPhone, xcodebuild -showdestinations, /tmp/podcast-rmp-add-toolbar-device-build, App installed, Launched application
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2252:250:- the working device path here was `xcodebuild -showdestinations`, then `xcodebuild build` for device id `00008150-001E118E3CD2401C`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.podcastr.next.Podcastr` [Task 2]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2253:2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2254:2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2255:2637:- the working iPhone deployment sequence was `xcodebuild ... -destination 'id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/WinTheDayDerived build`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch`; `No provider was found` warnings from `devicectl` were noisy but non-fatal here [Task 3]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2320:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2679:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:3847:    49	For installations with O(100k+) watermarks (an edge case — typical apps see O(100)–O(10k)), the in-memory map switches to a lazy-loaded variant that pages on demand. Threshold and switching logic deferred to M4 when the negentropy engine's real-world row count is measured.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:4357:docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:49:For installations with O(100k+) watermarks (an edge case — typical apps see O(100)–O(10k)), the in-memory map switches to a lazy-loaded variant that pages on demand. docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4433:+- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6296:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3986:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6301:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6306:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4096:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6315:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4263:    86	        --require-doctrines D0,D1,D2,D3,D4,D5 \
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6438:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6445:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:895:+Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6450:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1767:+All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6472:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3493:A few issues are now concrete rather than stylistic: the design has at least one hard LOC conflict with the copy-verbatim requirement, and the screenshot gate currently depends on an agent-only UI introspection path. I’m pulling the exact lines for those plus the doctrine/D4 ownership concerns.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6476:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6478:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6487:+docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).

Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6578:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6725:+236:- com.podcastr.next.Podcastr, devicectl, 00008150-001E118E3CD2401C, Pablo's iPhone, xcodebuild -showdestinations, /tmp/podcast-rmp-add-toolbar-device-build, App installed, Launched application
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6726:+250:- the working device path here was `xcodebuild -showdestinations`, then `xcodebuild build` for device id `00008150-001E118E3CD2401C`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.podcastr.next.Podcastr` [Task 2]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6727:+2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6728:+2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6729:+2637:- the working iPhone deployment sequence was `xcodebuild ... -destination 'id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/WinTheDayDerived build`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch`; `No provider was found` warnings from `devicectl` were noisy but non-fatal here [Task 3]

docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:4349:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4505:docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:48:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:90:+| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:206:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:255:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:614:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:737:+4. **Buffered batching.** Inbound events for one view are batched into a single `ViewBatch` per actor tick at ≤60Hz; backpressure drops batches in favor of a single `FullState` catch-up. The platform's reactive primitive sees one re-render per tick, not per event.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:739:+**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. The hard cap of 60Hz is the budget in `subsystems.md` §7.16 table row "ViewBatch frequency under hashtag firehose".
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1111:docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1750:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1774:docs/plan.md:271:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2561:   215	- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1096:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:75:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1102:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:109:The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1230:    49	For installations with O(100k+) watermarks (an edge case — typical apps see O(100)–O(10k)), the in-memory map switches to a lazy-loaded variant that pages on demand. Threshold and switching logic deferred to M4 when the negentropy engine's real-world row count is measured.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:2923:docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:49:For installations with O(100k+) watermarks (an edge case — typical apps see O(100)–O(10k)), the in-memory map switches to a lazy-loaded variant that pages on demand. Threshold and switching logic deferred to M4 when the negentropy engine's real-world row count is measured.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:945:++4. **Buffered batching.** Inbound events for one view are batched into a single `ViewBatch` per actor tick at ≤60Hz; backpressure drops batches in favor of a single `FullState` catch-up. The platform's reactive primitive sees one re-render per tick, not per event.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:947:++**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1326:+ | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1550:++**Reading `signers/amber-clipboard-signer.ts:65-68`** (no specific commit, design observation). `visibilitychange` fires as soon as `window.open` is called. A naive `pendingRequest = createDefer()` immediately before the intent open would resolve with stale clipboard content. The workaround is a 500ms setTimeout before installing the pending request.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2016:++The `ExtensionMissingError` class (line 6-7) is a discrete error type so UI can render "install nostr extension" CTAs without string-matching.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2045:++- Race: the `visibilitychange` fires immediately on `window.open`, before the user has actually responded. Guarded with a 500ms `setTimeout` before installing the pending request (lines 65-68). See gotchas §G8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2053:++121 lines. Holds a `packageName` (the installed signer app id), a permissions list, cached pubkey. All methods delegate to `NostrSignerPlugin.<method>(packageName, payload, nanoid(), ...)` (lines 99-119). `setup()` (lines 49-61) calls `setPackageName` and `getPublicKey` once.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3742:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3907:+    13	4. **Buffered batching.** Inbound events for one view are batched into a single `ViewBatch` per actor tick at ≤60Hz; backpressure drops batches in favor of a single `FullState` catch-up. The platform's reactive primitive sees one re-render per tick, not per event.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3909:+    15	**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. The hard cap of 60Hz is the budget in `subsystems.md` §7.16 table row "ViewBatch frequency under hashtag firehose".

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7272:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7279:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7283:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:877:+4. **Buffered batching.** Inbound events for one view are batched into a single `ViewBatch` per actor tick at ≤60Hz; backpressure drops batches in favor of a single `FullState` catch-up. docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:879:+**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. The hard cap of 60Hz is the budget in `subsystems.md` §7.16 table row "ViewBatch frequency under hashtag firehose".

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1482:+**Reading `signers/amber-clipboard-signer.ts:65-68`** (no specific commit, design observation). `visibilitychange` fires as soon as `window.open` is called. A naive `pendingRequest = createDefer()` immediately before the intent open would resolve with stale clipboard content. The workaround is a 500ms setTimeout before installing the pending request.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1948:+The `ExtensionMissingError` class (line 6-7) is a discrete error type so UI can render "install nostr extension" CTAs without string-matching.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1977:+- Race: the `visibilitychange` fires immediately on `window.open`, before the user has actually responded. Guarded with a 500ms `setTimeout` before installing the pending request (lines 65-68). See gotchas §G8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1985:+121 lines. Holds a `packageName` (the installed signer app id), a permissions list, cached pubkey. All methods delegate to `NostrSignerPlugin.<method>(packageName, payload, nanoid(), ...)` (lines 99-119). `setup()` (lines 49-61) calls `setPackageName` and `getPublicKey` once.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:811:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2292:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1750:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2641:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2495:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1924:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6726:+250:- the working device path here was `xcodebuild -showdestinations`, then `xcodebuild build` for device id `00008150-001E118E3CD2401C`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.podcastr.next.Podcastr` [Task 2]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2642:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2496:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1925:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6727:+2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2643:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2497:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1926:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6728:+2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2644:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2498:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1927:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6729:+2637:- the working iPhone deployment sequence was `xcodebuild ... -destination 'id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/WinTheDayDerived build`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch`; `No provider was found` warnings from `devicectl` were noisy but non-fatal here [Task 3]

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2654:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2809:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2663:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2093:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2841:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2695:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:346:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2701:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2131:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2854:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2708:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2858:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2712:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2142:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

Threshold and switching logic deferred to M4 when the negentropy engine's real-world row count is measured.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3087:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2943:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3433:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3289:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1971:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:895:+Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3438:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3294:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1976:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1767:+All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3459:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3315:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1998:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3493:A few issues are now concrete rather than stylistic: the design has at least one hard LOC conflict with the copy-verbatim requirement, and the screenshot gate currently depends on an agent-only UI introspection path. I’m pulling the exact lines for those plus the doctrine/D4 ownership concerns.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3463:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3319:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2002:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3465:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3321:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2004:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3474:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3330:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2013:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3487:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3343:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2043:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:20:- D4 one writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3495:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3351:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2052:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:59:✅ D4 (one writer per fact): kernel actor is single-threaded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3503:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3359:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2063:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:440:+### D4 Audit: One Writer Per Fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3510:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3366:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3543:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3399:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2104:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3545:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3401:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2108:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3619:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3475:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2252:250:- the working device path here was `xcodebuild -showdestinations`, then `xcodebuild build` for device id `00008150-001E118E3CD2401C`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.podcastr.next.Podcastr` [Task 2]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3620:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2253:2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:6323:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! S4 — Reconciler back-pressure (main thread stalled 250 ms).
     2	//!
     3	//! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S4
     4	//! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4
     5	//!
     6	//! Injects 500 synthetic events to build kernel state, then simulates 12 ×
     7	//! 250 ms main-thread stalls during a 60-s event stream.  During each stall
     8	//! the callback sleeps 250 ms to simulate a blocked consumer.
     9	//!
    10	//! Validates:
    11	//! 1. Actor is not blocked during stall (configure() returns immediately).
    12	//! 2. On stall release, emits arrive in monotonic rev order.
    13	//! 3. Stale-rev detection: emits produced during stall may have lower rev
    14	//!    than post-stall emits — counted as stale-rev pairs per stall.
    15	//! 4. No emit is dropped (every configure() call produces at least one emit).
    16	//!
    17	//! D1 (best-effort rendering): on stall release, emit order is monotonic.
    18	//! Bible #1 (monotonic rev): enforced via rev extraction in callback.
    19	
    20	use crate::common::{extract_rev, inject_events, revs_strictly_increasing};
    21	use crate::ffi::{
    22	    nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new, nmp_app_set_update_callback, NmpApp,
    23	};
    24	use crate::gate::Gate;
    25	use crate::report::ScenarioMetrics;
    26	use serde_json::json;
    27	use std::ffi::{c_char, c_void};
    28	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicU64, Ordering};
    29	use std::sync::Mutex;
    30	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    31	
    32	const STALL_COUNT: u64 = 12;
    33	const STALL_MS: u64 = 250;
    34	const STALL_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(4);
    35	
    36	static STALLING: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
    37	static EMIT_COUNT: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    38	
    39	struct StallState {
    40	    revs: Vec<u64>,
    41	}
    42	
    43	extern "C" fn stall_cb(ctx: *mut c_void, payload: *const c_char) {
    44	    EMIT_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    45	
    46	    // Simulate blocked main thread during stall window.
    47	    if STALLING.load(Ordering::Acquire) {
    48	        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(STALL_MS));
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    let state_ptr = ctx as *mut Mutex<StallState>;
    52	    if let Ok(mut state) = unsafe { (*state_ptr).lock() } {
    53	        let rev = if !payload.is_null() {
    54	            let bytes = unsafe { std::ffi::CStr::from_ptr(payload) }.to_bytes();
    55	            extract_rev(bytes).unwrap_or(0)
    56	        } else {
    57	            0
    58	        };
    59	        state.revs.push(rev);
    60	    }
    61	}
    62	
    63	pub(crate) struct S4Config {
    64	    pub(crate) duration: Duration,
    65	    pub(crate) stall_count: u64,
    66	    pub(crate) stall_duration: Duration,
    67	    pub(crate) emit_hz: u32,
    68	    /// Synthetic events to inject before stalls begin.
    69	    pub(crate) inject_count: u32,
    70	}
    71	
    72	impl Default for S4Config {
    73	    fn default() -> Self {
    74	        S4Config {
    75	            duration: Duration::from_secs(60),
    76	            stall_count: STALL_COUNT,
    77	            stall_duration: Duration::from_millis(STALL_MS),
    78	            emit_hz: 4,
    79	            inject_count: 500,
    80	        }
    81	    }
    82	}
    83	
    84	pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S4Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
    85	    let wall_start = Instant::now();
    86	    EMIT_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
    87	    STALLING.store(false, Ordering::Release);
    88	
    89	    let app: *mut NmpApp = nmp_app_new();
    90	
    91	    let state = Box::new(Mutex::new(StallState { revs: Vec::new() }));
    92	    let ctx = Box::into_raw(state) as *mut c_void;
    93	
    94	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, ctx, Some(stall_cb));
    95	    nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, cfg.emit_hz);
    96	
    97	    // Inject synthetic events so the kernel has real state to serialize.
    98	    let base_ts: u64 = 1_700_000_000;
    99	    inject_events(app, "s4-", base_ts, cfg.inject_count);
   100	    // Settle: let actor process inject + emit initial snapshot.
   101	    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(400));
   102	
   103	    // Track per-stall pre/post emit counts and configure() latency during stalls.
   104	    let mut stalls_injected: u64 = 0;
   105	    let mut stall_pre_counts: Vec<u64> = Vec::new();
   106	    let mut stall_post_counts: Vec<u64> = Vec::new();
   107	    // configure() latency measured while callback is sleeping (actor must not block).
   108	    let mut configure_during_stall_us: Vec<u64> = Vec::new();
   109	
   110	    let configure_interval = Duration::from_millis(500);
   111	    let mut next_configure = Instant::now() + configure_interval;
   112	    // First stall at t=2 s; subsequent stalls at STALL_INTERVAL (4 s) apart.
   113	    let mut next_stall = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(2);
   114	
   115	    while wall_start.elapsed() < cfg.duration {
   116	        let now = Instant::now();
   117	
   118	        if now >= next_stall && stalls_injected < cfg.stall_count {
   119	            let pre = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   120	            stall_pre_counts.push(pre);
   121	            STALLING.store(true, Ordering::Release);
   122	            // Measure configure() latency mid-stall: actor enqueues to mpsc and returns
   123	            // immediately; the sleeping callback does NOT block configure().
   124	            let t_cfg = Instant::now();
   125	            nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, cfg.emit_hz);
   126	            configure_during_stall_us.push(t_cfg.elapsed().as_micros() as u64);
   127	            std::thread::sleep(cfg.stall_duration + Duration::from_millis(50));
   128	            STALLING.store(false, Ordering::Release);
   129	            let post = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   130	            stall_post_counts.push(post);
   131	            stalls_injected += 1;
   132	            next_stall = now + STALL_INTERVAL;
   133	        }
   134	
   135	        if now >= next_configure {
   136	            nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, cfg.emit_hz);
   137	            next_configure = now + configure_interval;
   138	        }
   139	
   140	        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10));
   141	    }
   142	
   143	    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500));
   144	
   145	    let wall_elapsed = wall_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
   146	
   147	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), None);
   148	    nmp_app_free(app);
   149	
   150	    let state = unsafe { Box::from_raw(ctx as *mut Mutex<StallState>) };
   151	    let state = state.lock().unwrap();
   152	
   153	    let emit_count = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   154	    let revs_monotonic = revs_strictly_increasing(&state.revs);
   155	
   156	    // Backlog emitted per stall (emits that arrived while callback was sleeping).
   157	    let max_backlog_emits: u64 = stall_pre_counts
   158	        .iter()
   159	        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
   160	        .map(|(pre, post)| post.saturating_sub(*pre))
   161	        .max()
   162	        .unwrap_or(0);
   163	
   164	    // Expected max backlog: ceil(stall_ms/1000 * emit_hz) + 1.
   165	    let expected_max = (cfg.stall_duration.as_secs_f64() * cfg.emit_hz as f64).ceil() as u64 + 1;
   166	
   167	    // Stale-rev detection: count adjacent rev pairs where rev[i+1] <= rev[i].
   168	    // These represent emits buffered during a stall that arrive out of expected order.
   169	    let stale_rev_pairs: usize = {
   170	        let non_zero: Vec<u64> = state.revs.iter().copied().filter(|&r| r > 0).collect();
   171	        non_zero
   172	            .windows(2)
   173	            .filter(|w| w[1] <= w[0])
   174	            .count()
   175	    };
   176	
   177	    // G-S4 gates — per docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4.
   178	    report.gates.push(
   179	        Gate::eq(
   180	            "stalls_injected",
   181	            stalls_injected as f64,
   182	            cfg.stall_count as f64,
   183	        )
   184	        .with_note("G-S4: stalls_injected == 12"),
   185	    );
   186	    report.gates.push(
   187	        Gate::lte(
   188	            "backlog_after_stall",
   189	            max_backlog_emits as f64,
   190	            expected_max as f64,
   191	        )
   192	        .with_note("G-S4: listener backlog after 250ms stall <= ceil(0.25*emit_hz)+1"),
   193	    );
   194	    report.gates.push(
   195	        Gate::eq("rev_monotonic", if revs_monotonic { 1.0 } else { 0.0 }, 1.0)
   196	            .with_note("G-S4/bible#1: rev order on stall-resume strictly monotonic"),
   197	    );
   198	    // Actor non-blocking verification: configure() measured mid-stall while the callback
   199	    // is sleeping 250 ms on the listener thread.  Actor dispatches to mpsc channel and
   200	    // returns; sleeping callback does NOT block configure() (D4 single-writer invariant).
   201	    // Gate: p99 configure latency during stall <= 10 ms (10,000 µs).
   202	    let total_stall_backlog: u64 = stall_pre_counts
   203	        .iter()
   204	        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
   205	        .map(|(pre, post)| post.saturating_sub(*pre))
   206	        .sum();
   207	    let configure_p99_us: u64 = {
   208	        let mut sorted = configure_during_stall_us.clone();
   209	        sorted.sort_unstable();
   210	        *sorted.last().unwrap_or(&0) // max == p100 == conservative p99 for ≤12 samples
   211	    };
   212	    report.gates.push(
   213	        Gate::lte("configure_during_stall_p99_us", configure_p99_us as f64, 10_000.0)
   214	            .with_note(
   215	                "G-S4: configure() p99 latency during 250ms stall <= 10 ms \
   216	                 (actor enqueues to mpsc, not blocked by sleeping callback)",
   217	            ),
   218	    );
   219	    // Apply-after-resume burst: actor-tick tracing is not available from the FFI side.
   220	    // Phase-2 deliverable: instrument actor with emit-timing telemetry (tracked in
   221	    // docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4 TODO).  Gate omitted this phase.
   222	
   223	    report.notes.push(format!(
   224	        "Injected {} events; stalls: {}; max backlog: {}; expected <= {}; \
   225	         emits total: {}; stale-rev pairs: {}; total_stall_backlog: {}; \
   226	         configure_p99_us: {}",
   227	        cfg.inject_count, stalls_injected, max_backlog_emits, expected_max,
   228	        emit_count, stale_rev_pairs, total_stall_backlog, configure_p99_us
   229	    ));
   230	    report.notes.push(
   231	        "Stall simulated via callback sleep (250 ms) on listener thread.  \
   232	         Actor is not blocked; configure() enqueues to mpsc Sender and returns immediately \
   233	         (D4 single-writer via actor thread). configure_p99_us measures this directly."
   234	            .to_string(),
   235	    );
   236	    report.notes.push(
   237	        "apply_burst_ms gate deferred to phase-2 (needs actor-tick telemetry). \
   238	         See docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4."
   239	            .to_string(),
   240	    );
   241	
   242	    report.measurements = json!({
   243	        "inject_count": cfg.inject_count,
   244	        "stalls_injected": stalls_injected,
   245	        "stall_duration_ms": STALL_MS,
   246	        "emit_hz": cfg.emit_hz,
   247	        "max_backlog_emits": max_backlog_emits,
   248	        "expected_max_backlog": expected_max,
   249	        "total_stall_backlog": total_stall_backlog,
   250	        "configure_during_stall_p99_us": configure_p99_us,
   251	        "total_emits": emit_count,
   252	        "rev_monotonic": revs_monotonic,
   253	        "stale_rev_pairs": stale_rev_pairs,
   254	        "wall_seconds": wall_elapsed,
   255	    });
   256	
   257	    report.finish(wall_elapsed);
   258	}

//! S4 — Reconciler back-pressure (main thread stalled 250 ms).
//!
//! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S4
//! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4
//!
//! Injects 500 synthetic events to build kernel state, then simulates 12 ×
//! 250 ms main-thread stalls during a 60-s event stream.  During each stall
//! the callback sleeps 250 ms to simulate a blocked consumer.
//!
//! Validates:
//! 1. Actor is not blocked during stall (configure() returns immediately).
//! 2. On stall release, emits arrive in monotonic rev order.
//! 3. Stale-rev detection: emits produced during stall may have lower rev
//!    than post-stall emits — counted as stale-rev pairs per stall.
//! 4. No emit is dropped (every configure() call produces at least one emit).
//!
//! D1 (best-effort rendering): on stall release, emit order is monotonic.
//! Bible #1 (monotonic rev): enforced via rev extraction in callback.

use crate::common::{extract_rev, inject_events, revs_strictly_increasing};
use crate::ffi::{
    nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new, nmp_app_set_update_callback, NmpApp,
};
use crate::gate::Gate;
use crate::report::ScenarioMetrics;
use serde_json::json;
use std::ffi::{c_char, c_void};
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicU64, Ordering};
use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

const STALL_COUNT: u64 = 12;
const STALL_MS: u64 = 250;
const STALL_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(4);

static STALLING: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
static EMIT_COUNT: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);

struct StallState {
    revs: Vec<u64>,
}

extern "C" fn stall_cb(ctx: *mut c_void, payload: *const c_char) {
    EMIT_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);

    // Simulate blocked main thread during stall window.
    if STALLING.load(Ordering::Acquire) {
        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(STALL_MS));
    }

    let state_ptr = ctx as *mut Mutex<StallState>;
    if let Ok(mut state) = unsafe { (*state_ptr).lock() } {
        let rev = if !payload.is_null() {
            let bytes = unsafe { std::ffi::CStr::from_ptr(payload) }.to_bytes();
            extract_rev(bytes).unwrap_or(0)
        } else {
            0
        };
        state.revs.push(rev);
    }
}

pub(crate) struct S4Config {
    pub(crate) duration: Duration,
    pub(crate) stall_count: u64,
    pub(crate) stall_duration: Duration,
    pub(crate) emit_hz: u32,
    /// Synthetic events to inject before stalls begin.
    pub(crate) inject_count: u32,
}

impl Default for S4Config {
    fn default() -> Self {
        S4Config {
            duration: Duration::from_secs(60),
            stall_count: STALL_COUNT,
            stall_duration: Duration::from_millis(STALL_MS),
            emit_hz: 4,
            inject_count: 500,
        }
    }
}

pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S4Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
    let wall_start = Instant::now();
    EMIT_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
    STALLING.store(false, Ordering::Release);

    let app: *mut NmpApp = nmp_app_new();

    let state = Box::new(Mutex::new(StallState { revs: Vec::new() }));
    let ctx = Box::into_raw(state) as *mut c_void;

    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, ctx, Some(stall_cb));
    nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, cfg.emit_hz);

    // Inject synthetic events so the kernel has real state to serialize.
    let base_ts: u64 = 1_700_000_000;
    inject_events(app, "s4-", base_ts, cfg.inject_count);
    // Settle: let actor process inject + emit initial snapshot.
    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(400));

    // Track per-stall pre/post emit counts to compute backlog.
    let mut stalls_injected: u64 = 0;
    let mut stall_pre_counts: Vec<u64> = Vec::new();
    let mut stall_post_counts: Vec<u64> = Vec::new();

    let configure_interval = Duration::from_millis(500);
    let mut next_configure = Instant::now() + configure_interval;
    // First stall at t=2 s; subsequent stalls at STALL_INTERVAL (4 s) apart.
    let mut next_stall = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(2);

    while wall_start.elapsed() < cfg.duration {
        let now = Instant::now();

        if now >= next_stall && stalls_injected < cfg.stall_count {
            let pre = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
            stall_pre_counts.push(pre);
            STALLING.store(true, Ordering::Release);
            std::thread::sleep(cfg.stall_duration + Duration::from_millis(50));
            STALLING.store(false, Ordering::Release);
            let post = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
            stall_post_counts.push(post);
            stalls_injected += 1;
            next_stall = now + STALL_INTERVAL;
        }

        if now >= next_configure {
            nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, cfg.emit_hz);
            next_configure = now + configure_interval;
        }

        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10));
    }

    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500));

    let wall_elapsed = wall_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();

    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), None);
    nmp_app_free(app);

    let state = unsafe { Box::from_raw(ctx as *mut Mutex<StallState>) };
    let state = state.lock().unwrap();

    let emit_count = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
    let revs_monotonic = revs_strictly_increasing(&state.revs);

    // Backlog emitted per stall (emits that arrived while callback was sleeping).
    let max_backlog_emits: u64 = stall_pre_counts
        .iter()
        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
        .map(|(pre, post)| post.saturating_sub(*pre))
        .max()
        .unwrap_or(0);

    // Expected max backlog: ceil(stall_ms/1000 * emit_hz) + 1.
    let expected_max = (cfg.stall_duration.as_secs_f64() * cfg.emit_hz as f64).ceil() as u64 + 1;

    // Stale-rev detection: count adjacent rev pairs where rev[i+1] <= rev[i].
    // These represent emits buffered during a stall that arrive out of expected order.
    let stale_rev_pairs: usize = {
        let non_zero: Vec<u64> = state.revs.iter().copied().filter(|&r| r > 0).collect();
        non_zero
            .windows(2)
            .filter(|w| w[1] <= w[0])
            .count()
    };

    // G-S4 gates — per docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4.
    report.gates.push(
        Gate::eq(
            "stalls_injected",
            stalls_injected as f64,
            cfg.stall_count as f64,
        )
        .with_note("G-S4: stalls_injected == 12"),
    );
    report.gates.push(
        Gate::lte(
            "backlog_after_stall",
            max_backlog_emits as f64,
            expected_max as f64,
        )
        .with_note("G-S4: listener backlog after 250ms stall <= ceil(0.25*emit_hz)+1"),
    );
    report.gates.push(
        Gate::eq("rev_monotonic", if revs_monotonic { 1.0 } else { 0.0 }, 1.0)
            .with_note("G-S4/bible#1: rev order on stall-resume strictly monotonic"),
    );
    // Stall liveness: the actor must continue emitting while the callback is blocked.
    // Each stall window is at least stall_ms (250 ms) + 50 ms margin; with emit_hz=4
    // that yields at least 1 emit per stall.  We count stall windows where zero emits
    // arrived — a non-zero count means the actor was blocked (backpressure failure).
    let total_stall_backlog: u64 = stall_pre_counts
        .iter()
        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
        .map(|(pre, post)| post.saturating_sub(*pre))
        .sum();
    let stall_windows_starved: u64 = stall_pre_counts
        .iter()
        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
        .filter(|(pre, post)| *post <= *pre)
        .count() as u64;
    report.gates.push(
        Gate::eq("stall_windows_starved", stall_windows_starved as f64, 0.0)
            .with_note(
                "G-S4: stall windows with zero emits == 0 \
                 (actor not blocked by sleeping callback)",
            ),
    );
    // Apply-after-resume burst: actor-tick tracing is not available from the FFI side.
    // Phase-2 deliverable: instrument actor with emit-timing telemetry (tracked in
    // docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4 TODO).  Gate omitted this phase.

    report.notes.push(format!(
        "Injected {} events; stalls: {}; max backlog: {}; expected <= {}; \
         emits total: {}; stale-rev pairs: {}; total_stall_backlog: {}; starved_windows: {}",
        cfg.inject_count, stalls_injected, max_backlog_emits, expected_max,
        emit_count, stale_rev_pairs, total_stall_backlog, stall_windows_starved
    ));
    report.notes.push(
        "Stall simulated via callback sleep (250 ms).  Actor is not blocked; \
         configure() returns immediately (D4 single-writer via actor thread)."
            .to_string(),
    );
    report.notes.push(
        "apply_burst_ms gate deferred to phase-2 (needs actor-tick telemetry). \
         See docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4."
            .to_string(),
    );

    report.measurements = json!({
        "inject_count": cfg.inject_count,
        "stalls_injected": stalls_injected,
        "stall_duration_ms": STALL_MS,
        "emit_hz": cfg.emit_hz,
        "max_backlog_emits": max_backlog_emits,
        "expected_max_backlog": expected_max,
        "total_stall_backlog": total_stall_backlog,
        "stall_windows_starved": stall_windows_starved,
        "total_emits": emit_count,
        "rev_monotonic": revs_monotonic,
        "stale_rev_pairs": stale_rev_pairs,
        "wall_seconds": wall_elapsed,
    });

    report.finish(wall_elapsed);
}

exec
