 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# FFI hardening — scenarios (§3)
     2	
     3	Eleven named failure modes. Each entry: setup, assertion, threading target,
     4	runner (Rust harness / XCUITest / Sonnet-agent), and numeric threshold.
     5	Numeric thresholds are quoted here for clarity; the canonical exit-gate
     6	table lives in [`gates.md`](./gates.md).
     7	
     8	Conventions:
     9	- "dispatch" = a single Swift→Rust call through any `nmp_app_*` symbol.
    10	- "emit" = one full update payload delivered to the registered callback.
    11	- "view-handle wrapper" = the iOS-side refcounted entity created when a
    12	  SwiftUI component mounts a `useProfile(pubkey)`-style observer (in the
    13	  current `NmpStress` codebase this maps to `KernelModel.claimProfile` +
    14	  `releaseProfile`; ADR-0005).
    15	
    16	---
    17	
    18	## S1. Mount/unmount churn — view-handle wrapper refcount
    19	
    20	**The bug shape.** Wrappers (`claim_profile` / `release_profile`) leak
    21	internal references; the kernel's per-pubkey refcount table grows
    22	without bound; eventually the platform shadow holds entries no
    23	component still needs.
    24	
    25	**Setup.** Drive the kernel with a closed-loop of 1,000
    26	claim/release pairs per second across 100 unique pubkeys (rotating
    27	LRU). Sustain for **10 minutes** (600,000 cycles). Each cycle:
    28	`claim_profile(pk_i, consumer_id_i) → 1ms wait → release_profile(pk_i, consumer_id_i)`.
    29	
    30	**Threading.** Driver thread = Swift main (XCUITest) **or** the
    31	single-threaded executor in the Rust harness. Actor thread receives
    32	the storm; listener emits batched updates.
    33	
    34	**Assertions.**
    35	1. Final refcount for every pubkey = 0.
    36	2. `Kernel::interest_refcount(pk).is_none()` for every pubkey after a
    37	   grace-period (configurable, default 30 s — ADR-0005).
    38	3. Process RSS after 10 min ≤ baseline + **5 MB**.
    39	4. Instruments → Leaks → 0 retained-by-cycle leaks.
    40	5. Allocator (counting allocator from `reactivity-bench`) shows
    41	   slope ≤ 0 bytes/sec post-warmup (first 30 s).
    42	
    43	**Runner.** Primary: Rust harness `ffi-stress mount-unmount-churn`.
    44	Secondary (nightly only): XCUITest `S1MountUnmountChurn.swift` driving
    45	real `NmpStress` with Instruments-Leaks attached.
    46	
    47	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S1.
    48	
    49	---
    50	
    51	## S2. Dispatch flood
    52	
    53	**The bug shape.** Swift dispatches faster than the actor can drain;
    54	the mpsc channel grows unbounded; OOM eventually. Or: the FFI symbol
    55	itself takes a lock that blocks the main thread.
    56	
    57	**Setup.** **10,000 dispatches per second** for 60 s across **N=4**
    58	caller threads (matching iOS's typical concurrency budget). Mix:
    59	30 % `open_author` (valid pubkey from a pool of 50), 30 %
    60	`close_author`, 20 % `claim_profile`, 20 % `release_profile`. All
    61	inputs valid (no validation-path flooding — that's S7).
    62	
    63	**Threading.** Four caller OS threads (in the Rust harness, four
    64	`std::thread`; in the XCUITest variant, four `DispatchQueue.global()`
    65	queues). Actor thread is the bottleneck under test.
    66	
    67	**Assertions.**
    68	1. No dispatch call takes > **1 ms** at p99 (Swift→Rust channel send
    69	   latency; this is the bible-#3 "fire-and-forget" guarantee
    70	   quantified).
    71	2. Main thread (XCUITest variant) is never blocked > **16 ms**
    72	   (measured via XCTest's `XCTHitchMetric`).
    73	3. Actor mpsc backlog never exceeds **10,000 messages** (configurable
    74	   harness-side check via `actor_queue_depth` field already exposed in
    75	   `KernelMetrics`).
    76	4. Zero dropped messages: count of `_ = tx.send(...)` failures = 0.
    77	5. RSS growth over 60 s ≤ 20 MB.
    78	
    79	**Runner.** Rust harness primary. XCUITest secondary because the
    80	real-world iOS main-thread budget is the load-bearing constraint.
    81	
    82	---
    83	
    84	## S3. Snapshot pressure — `AppUpdate::FullState` with 100k events
    85	
    86	**The bug shape.** Marshal cost for a full-state snapshot grows
    87	super-linearly; the listener thread spends > 1 emit-interval
    88	serializing JSON; the reconciler exceeds 60 Hz; the iOS main thread
    89	spends every frame applying snapshots.
    90	
    91	**Setup.** Pre-load kernel state via a captured firehose-bench trace
    92	(`docs/perf/firehose-bench/traces/m10.5-snapshot.cap`, captured
    93	expressly for this scenario) containing **100,000 stored events**
    94	across 3,000 authors. Force a full-state emit by calling
    95	`nmp_app_configure` (which currently triggers `emit_now`) ten times
    96	in a row to amortize the JSON cost over ten observations.
    97	
    98	**Threading.** Actor builds the update; listener serializes; Swift
    99	main applies. The bottleneck under test is the **listener
   100	serialization + main-thread apply** path.
   101	
   102	**Assertions.**
   103	1. Per-emit JSON serialization wall-time p99 ≤ **20 ms** (Rust side).
   104	2. Payload size ≤ **2 MiB** (if it exceeds, the harness fails and
   105	   asks for bible-#10 granular-update variants).
   106	3. Swift `apply_us` (already instrumented in `KernelModel.apply`) p99
   107	   ≤ **16 ms**.
   108	4. End-to-end reconciler frequency stays ≤ **60 Hz** (configured cap
   109	   is 12 Hz today; the harness verifies the cap is honored under
   110	   pressure rather than asserting the cap value itself).
   111	5. Allocations per emit ≤ **payload_bytes × 2** (no quadratic-copy
   112	   regression).
   113	
   114	**Runner.** Rust harness primary (measures Rust-side); XCUITest
   115	secondary (measures Swift-side).
   116	
   117	---
   118	
   119	## S4. Reconciler back-pressure — main thread stalled 250 ms
   120	
   121	**The bug shape.** When the iOS main thread blocks (e.g.,
   122	file-picker, modal sheet, large layout pass), the listener-thread
   123	emits accumulate. On resume, the app applies a flood and visibly
   124	hitches, or worse, the actor stalls because its `update_tx` channel
   125	fills.
   126	
   127	**Setup.** Start the kernel, open following + author + thread views,
   128	drive 100 events/sec for 60 s. Periodically inject a **250 ms
   129	synchronous sleep on the main thread** (XCUITest:
   130	`Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 0.25)` inside the reconciler closure).
   131	Repeat every 5 s for the 60-s window → **12 stalls total**.
   132	
   133	**Threading.** Main thread (artificially stalled); listener thread
   134	(accumulates emits); actor (must not block on `update_tx.send`).
   135	
   136	**Assertions.**
   137	1. Actor `actor_queue_depth` never grows during a stall (the listener
   138	   thread is the queue under back-pressure, not the actor's command
   139	   queue).
   140	2. Listener `update_rx` backlog after each 250 ms stall ≤ ⌈ 250 ms
   141	   × emit_hz ⌉ + 1 = **2** messages (at 4 Hz default) or **4** (at 12
   142	   Hz max).
   143	3. On stall release, the main thread applies all backlogged emits in
   144	   monotonic `rev` order (bible #1).
   145	4. Stale-rev filter in `KernelModel.apply` correctly drops any
   146	   intermediate revs (already implemented at KernelModel.swift:139;
   147	   harness validates).
   148	5. No emit is dropped — the listener delivers each one in order.
   149	
   150	**Runner.** XCUITest only (the bug shape is iOS-main-thread specific).
   151	
   152	---
   153	
   154	## S5. Reentrancy — dispatch from inside reconciler callback
   155	
   156	**The bug shape.** A SwiftUI `.onChange` handler observes a kernel
   157	update and immediately calls `kernel.openAuthor(...)`. If the FFI
   158	symbol takes a lock the listener thread also takes, deadlock. If it
   159	re-enters synchronously, message ordering can invert.
   160	
   161	**Setup.** Register a callback that, on every emit where
   162	`update.metrics.eventsSinceLastUpdate > 0`, immediately dispatches
   163	`open_author` for the first item in the timeline. Sustain for 30 s
   164	with a 50 events/sec inflow.
   165	
   166	**Threading.** Callback runs on **listener thread**; dispatch
   167	enqueues to the actor's command channel which the **actor thread**
   168	drains. The bug shape requires the actor to be processing a message
   169	when the callback fires (race window).
   170	
   171	**Assertions.**
   172	1. Zero deadlocks (harness times out after 5 s = fail).
   173	2. Message order preserved — for every emit-then-dispatch pair, the
   174	   resulting `OpenAuthor` action is processed strictly **after** the
   175	   emit that triggered it (verified via `rev` monotonicity in the
   176	   subsequent emit referencing that author view).
   177	3. No dispatch loss under reentrant pressure.
   178	4. Listener thread CPU time per emit ≤ **2 ms** even with the
   179	   reentrant dispatch path active.
   180	
   181	**Runner.** Rust harness primary, XCUITest secondary.
   182	
   183	---
   184	
   185	Scenarios S6–S11 (capability storms, error exhaustion, planner DOS,
   186	relay flap, long suspend, memory RSS instrumentation) are in
   187	[`scenarios-detail.md`](./scenarios-detail.md).

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# FFI hardening — exit gates (§5) and doctrine review (§8)
     2	
     3	Two tables in this file:
     4	1. **§G** numeric exit gates per scenario per metric — the complete
     5	   contract; every cell is a specific number with units. No "fast
     6	   enough", no "acceptable".
     7	2. **§D** doctrine D0–D5 review checklist — each line item maps to
     8	   a scenario, an audit document, or an explicit code-comment task.
     9	
    10	The values here are the **simulator** baseline (iPhone 16 Pro
    11	simulator on Apple Silicon Mac). The iPhone 12 hardware baseline is
    12	quoted as a separate column where scaling matters; the per-device
    13	coefficient is documented in
    14	`docs/perf/m10.5/iphone12-baseline.md` (produced by the M1–M10
    15	firehose-bench rerun, not by this design doc).
    16	
    17	---
    18	
    19	## §G. Numeric exit gates
    20	
    21	### G-S1. Mount/unmount churn (10 min)
    22	
    23	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    24	|---|---|---|---|
    25	| Wall duration | `==` | 600 s ± 5 s | 600 s ± 5 s |
    26	| Cycles completed | `>=` | 540,000 (90 % of 600k nominal) | 360,000 (60 %) |
    27	| Final per-pubkey refcount | `==` | 0 for every key | 0 |
    28	| Unmatched claim/release | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    29	| Process RSS growth | `<=` | 5 MiB | 8 MiB |
    30	| Counting allocator slope (post-warmup) | `<=` | 0 bytes/sec | 0 bytes/sec |
    31	| Instruments retained-by-cycle leaks | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    32	| Listener thread CPU avg | `<=` | 5 % | 8 % |
    33	
    34	### G-S2. Dispatch flood (60 s, 10 000/s, 4 threads)
    35	
    36	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    37	|---|---|---|---|
    38	| Dispatches submitted | `>=` | 600,000 | 600,000 |
    39	| Per-dispatch Swift→Rust send latency p50 | `<=` | 100 µs | 200 µs |
    40	| Per-dispatch Swift→Rust send latency p99 | `<=` | 1 ms | 2 ms |
    41	| Actor mpsc backlog peak | `<=` | 10,000 | 10,000 |
    42	| Main thread hitches > 16 ms (XCTHitchMetric) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    43	| Dropped messages (Sender::send failures) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    44	| RSS growth | `<=` | 20 MiB | 30 MiB |
    45	
    46	### G-S3. Snapshot pressure (100 k events, 10 emits)
    47	
    48	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    49	|---|---|---|---|
    50	| Per-emit JSON serialization wall-time p99 (Rust) | `<=` | 20 ms | 40 ms |
    51	| Per-emit payload size | `<=` | 2 MiB | 2 MiB |
    52	| Swift `apply_us` p99 | `<=` | 16 ms (16 000 µs) | 32 ms |
    53	| End-to-end reconciler frequency | `<=` | 60 Hz | 60 Hz |
    54	| Allocations per emit | `<=` | `2 × payload_bytes` | `2 × payload_bytes` |
    55	| Counting allocator: temporary peak during emit | `<=` | `3 × payload_bytes` | `3 × payload_bytes` |
    56	| `rev` monotonicity across the 10 emits | `==` | strictly increasing | strictly increasing |
    57	
    58	### G-S4. Reconciler back-pressure (12 stalls × 250 ms, 60 s)
    59	
    60	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    61	|---|---|---|---|
    62	| Stalls injected | `==` | 12 | 12 |
    63	| Actor `actor_queue_depth` peak during any stall | `<=` | 50 | 100 |
    64	| Listener `update_rx` backlog after each stall | `<=` | `ceil(0.25 × emit_hz) + 1` | same |
    65	| Rev order on resume | `==` | strictly monotonic | strictly monotonic |
    66	| Stale-rev filter drops counted | `>=` | 1 per stall (12 total) | same |
    67	| Total emits dropped (listener-side) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    68	| Apply-after-resume burst max | `<=` | 33 ms | 50 ms |
    69	
    70	### G-S5. Reentrancy (30 s, 50 events/s, callback dispatches)
    71	
    72	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    73	|---|---|---|---|
    74	| Deadlocks (5 s watchdog) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    75	| Dispatch-from-callback emits processed | `>=` | 100 (over 30 s) | 100 |
    76	| Out-of-order callback→dispatch pairs | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    77	| Listener thread CPU per emit avg | `<=` | 2 ms | 4 ms |
    78	| Dispatch loss | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    79	
    80	### G-S6. Capability lifecycle storms (1 000 cycles, 5 min)
    81	
    82	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    83	|---|---|---|---|
    84	| Cycles completed | `==` | 1,000 | 1,000 |
    85	| Thread count delta from baseline | `<=` | 2 | 2 |
    86	| Active `RelayControl` entries after final Stop | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    87	| Relay-worker generation counter wrap | `==` | 0 (no wrap) | 0 |
    88	| RSS growth | `<=` | 2 MiB | 4 MiB |
    89	| Cycle wall-time p99 | `<=` | 250 ms | 500 ms |
    90	| Deadlocks (5 s per-cycle watchdog) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    91	
    92	### G-S7. Error-shape exhaustion (full matrix)
    93	
    94	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    95	|---|---|---|---|
    96	| Crashes / signals across full input matrix | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    97	| Crashes on NULL app pointer calls | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    98	| Toast field populated on every silent-no-op path | `==` | 100 % of validation-fail paths | 100 % |
    99	| Toast strings non-empty + actionable (regex match) | `==` | 100 % | 100 % |
   100	| Instruments-Allocations delta over full matrix | `==` | 0 (no leak per error path) | 0 |
   101	| Symbols × invalid-input variants exercised | `>=` | 70 (14 symbols × 5 variants avg) | 70 |
   102	
   103	### G-S8. Subscription planner DOS (5 × 10 k storm, 30 s gap)
   104	
   105	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
   106	|---|---|---|---|
   107	| Peak working-set RSS during storm | `<=` | 150 MiB | 200 MiB |
   108	| Wire-REQ frame count per 10 k OpenViews | `<=` | 2,500 (4× dedup floor) | 2,500 |
   109	| Planner state size after all closes | `<=` | baseline × 1.05 | baseline × 1.10 |
   110	| Actor stalls > 50 ms during storm | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   111	| Auto-close success rate (every release → close) | `==` | 100 % | 100 % |
   112	| Wire subscription leaks after 5 storms | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   113	
   114	### G-S9. Relay flap (100/min × 10 min = 1 000 flaps)
   115	
   116	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
   117	|---|---|---|---|
   118	| Flaps injected | `==` | 1,000 | 1,000 |
   119	| Wire-subscription count after each reconnect | `==` | 8 (one per logical view) | 8 |
   120	| `reconnect_count` field matches injected count | `==` | 1,000 | 1,000 |
   121	| Reconnect→first-REQ-out p99 latency | `<=` | 500 ms | 1,000 ms |
   122	| Total bytes-RX over 10 min | `<=` | 2 × baseline bandwidth | 2 × baseline |
   123	| Deferred-outbound queue drain on reconnect | `==` | 100 % | 100 % |
   124	| Subscription leaks (count after final reconnect) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   125	
   126	### G-S10. Long suspend (conditional on M3+M4)
   127	
   128	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
   129	|---|---|---|---|
   130	| Watermark persisted across suspend | `==` | true | true |
   131	| Catch-up REQ uses `since` filter | `==` | true | true |
   132	| Catch-up window wall-time | `<=` | 5 s | 8 s |
   133	| Catch-up bandwidth vs steady-state | `<=` | 3× | 3× |
   134	| Post-resume state snapshot equals control | `==` | byte-equal (relevant payloads) | byte-equal |
   135	
   136	### G-S11. Memory RSS instrumentation — iPhone 12 baseline
   137	
   138	*M1 RSS-measurement deferral honored here (T22). Simulator column is the Linux Rust-harness
   139	CI regression check; iPhone 12 column is the load-bearing gate.*
   140	
   141	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold (Linux Rust harness) | iPhone 12 threshold |
   142	|---|---|---|---|
   143	| RSS at stabilisation (100 active views) | `<=` | 100 MiB (ADR-0003 working-set gate) | 150 MiB |
   144	| RSS growth slope post-warmup (first 30 s) | `<=` | 0 bytes/sec | 0 bytes/sec |
   145	| Post-shutdown RSS delta | `<=` | 5 MiB | 10 MiB |
   146	| Instruments → Leaks count | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   147	| RSS sample interval | `==` | 5 s | 5 s |
   148	
   149	---
   150	
   151	## §G.1 Aggregate gates (apply across all scenarios)
   152	
   153	| Metric | Op | Threshold | Source |
   154	|---|---|---|---|
   155	| Total Instruments-Leaks across the suite | `==` | 0 | every scenario with `--instruments` |
   156	| Total crashes / panics across the suite | `==` | 0 | every scenario |
   157	| M1–M10 firehose-bench rerun regression (p99 ms, any scenario) | `<=` | +5 % vs baseline | plan.md §M10.5 exit-gate row 2 |
   158	| Pre-merge runtime budget per PR | `<=` | 15 min wall-time on `macos-14` | ci.md §1 |
   159	| Nightly runtime budget on Mac mini self-hosted | `<=` | 90 min | ci.md §2 |
   160	
   161	---
   162	
   163	## §D. Doctrine D0–D5 review checklist
   164	
   165	Cardinal doctrines per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5.
   166	Each line item names the artifact that proves it.
   167	
   168	> **Note.** The task brief mentioned "D0–D5". The canonical list in
   169	> the spec **is exactly six items: D0, D1, D2, D3, D4, D5.** This
   170	> file follows that list. (The aim.md §6 list of 12 items is a
   171	> larger doctrine set the FFI hardening pass deliberately does
   172	> not redundantly re-prove — items beyond D0–D5 are covered by
   173	> earlier milestones' own gates.)
   174	
   175	### D0. Kernel never grows app nouns
   176	
   177	- ✅ **Proof:** [debt-inventory.md §3 D0 audit](../../perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md) — verified
   178	  no app-domain types in `nmp-core` substrate.
   179	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S6 (capability lifecycle storms) does
   180	  1 000 start/stop/restart cycles; the kernel's capability set is
   181	  unchanged across all cycles (no dynamic registration of new
   182	  noun types).
   183	- 📝 **Sign-off:** auditor signature line in
   184	  `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md` § D0.
   185	
   186	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
   187	
   188	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S3 (snapshot pressure) — every emit must
   189	  satisfy the placeholder-then-refine contract: missing display
   190	  names → shortened-npub placeholders are present (no `None`); when
   191	  kind:0 arrives, the next emit updates in place. Assertion: for
   192	  every author with placeholder text in emit N, emit M > N where
   193	  the kind:0 arrived must contain the resolved text and an
   194	  unchanged `id`.
   195	- ✅ **Stress proof (back-pressure path):** S4 (reconciler
   196	  back-pressure) — on stall release, the timeline renders with
   197	  placeholders immediately, not spinner-blocked. Emit ordering is
   198	  monotonic; no frame is dropped.
   199	- ✅ **Code proof:** iOS `ProfileCard.placeholder(pubkey:)` renders
   200	  immediately from `debt-inventory.md` §3 D1 audit; no spinner, no
   201	  `nil` guard blocking render.
   202	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D1.
   203	
   204	> **Note on S10.** S10 (long suspend) would provide additional D1
   205	> evidence for the resume path, but S10 is conditional on M3+M4
   206	> (persistence + watermarks), which are not complete at M10.5 close.
   207	> S10 is deferred to M11.5 or whenever M3+M4 land; it is not used as
   208	> doctrine sign-off evidence here. D1 is signed off on S3 + S4 + code
   209	> proof above.
   210	
   211	### D2. Reactivity contract — composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound
   212	
   213	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S2 (dispatch flood), S3 (snapshot pressure),
   214	  S8 (planner DOS) all assert reconciler frequency stays bounded
   215	  (currently capped at 12 Hz; the doctrine says ≤ 60 Hz; the gate
   216	  is the 60 Hz ceiling, the configured cap is internal policy).
   217	- ✅ **Stress proof (working set):** S8 asserts planner state
   218	  returns to baseline ± 5 % after close storms.
   219	- ✅ **Composite reverse index:** S8 asserts wire-REQ dedup
   220	  (4× compaction floor); the reverse index drives the dedup.
   221	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D2.
   222	
   223	### D3. Errors never cross FFI
   224	
   225	- ⚠️ **Current state:** debt-inventory §3 D3 audit concludes
   226	  errors-as-data crosses FFI correctly via `RelayStatus.last_error`
   227	  / `last_notice`. App-action error paths are
   228	  **silently no-op** (see parent doc §7.2).
   229	- ✅ **Stress proof + remediation:** S7 (error-shape exhaustion)
   230	  exercises every invalid-input path. The M10.5 deliverable
   231	  adds a `toast: Option<String>` field to the JSON update
   232	  payload and populates it from S7's failure paths. The
   233	  schema change is additive and non-breaking.
   234	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D3, with the explicit
   235	  note that this milestone *closes* the D3-incomplete state
   236	  identified in the debt inventory.
   237	
   238	### D4. Single writer per fact — caches derive
   239	
   240	- ✅ **Proof:** debt-inventory §3 D4 audit — single-threaded actor
   241	  is the only writer; `KernelModel` on the iOS side is
   242	  `@MainActor`-isolated so derived state has a single writer per
   243	  layer.
   244	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S5 (reentrancy) — the reentrant
   245	  dispatch-from-callback path does not violate single-writer
   246	  because the dispatch enqueues a command for the actor; the
   247	  callback does not mutate kernel state directly.
   248	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S1 (mount/unmount) — refcount table is
   249	  only mutated by the actor; verified by harness asserting no
   250	  refcount-table mutation on any other thread (via
   251	  `loom`-style instrumentation in the harness binary only, not
   252	  prod).
   253	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D4.
   254	
   255	### D5. Capabilities report, never decide
   256	
   257	Canonical (plan.md:9): capabilities surface position events to the
   258	iOS layer; **no policy decisions are made at the bridge**.
   259	
   260	- ✅ **Code proof:** `CapabilityModule` trait (`substrate/capability.rs`)
   261	  defines typed `Request`/`Result` pairs — modules *report* capability
   262	  results back to the platform; they never decide what to do with them.
   263	  The `callback_interface_name()` entry point delivers results to the
   264	  iOS layer as data, not as control signals.
   265	- ✅ **Capability evidence (M10.5 surface):** The relay-role capability
   266	  (content + indexer) is the only active module today. `RelayStatus` is
   267	  emitted as an update field — the kernel reports relay position; iOS
   268	  renders it. No routing decisions are made at the bridge.
   269	- ✅ **M11 prep evidence:** Both `AudioPlaybackCapability` and
   270	  `EmbeddingCapability` (planned for M11) follow the same pattern: the
   271	  kernel emits position/ready events back to the platform; the platform
   272	  renders them. No capability module will acquire routing authority.
   273	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S6 (capability lifecycle storms) — 1,000
   274	  start/stop/restart cycles verify the relay capability lifecycle without
   275	  any module gaining decision authority; all `RelayControl` entries are
   276	  closed after every Stop.
   277	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D5.
   278	
   279	---
   280	
   281	## §D.1 Doctrine sign-off artifact
   282	
   283	`docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md` is produced at the *end* of
   284	M10.5, not as part of this design. The structure is:
   285	
   286	```
   287	# M10.5 Doctrine Review
   288	
   289	| Doctrine | Status | Evidence | Reviewer | Date |
   290	|---|---|---|---|---|
   291	| D0 | PASS | debt-inventory §3 D0 + S6 metrics.json | <name> | <date> |
   292	| D1 | PASS | S3 + S4 metrics.json + S3/screenshots (placeholder-then-refine path) | <name> | <date> |
   293	| D2 | PASS | S2/S3/S8 metrics.json | <name> | <date> |
   294	| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
   295	| D4 | PASS | debt-inventory §3 D4 + S5/S1 metrics.json | <name> | <date> |
   296	| D5 | PASS | debt-inventory §3 D5 + S6 metrics.json + capability.rs code review | <name> | <date> |
   297	
   298	## Notes
   299	<any caveats, deferrals, follow-ups>
   300	```
   301	
   302	A PASS in every row + the §7.1 grep gate yielding 0 hits = M10.5
   303	ready to close. Anything less = the milestone is open.

     1	//! ffi-stress — FFI hardening stress harness (M10.5 phase 1).
     2	//!
     3	//! Drives the `nmp_app_*` C symbols via extern declarations (same ABI Swift
     4	//! uses) and verifies S1–S5 gate conditions from
     5	//! `docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md`.
     6	//!
     7	//! Usage:
     8	//!   ffi-stress <scenario> [--duration <D>] [--threads <N>] [--fail-on-gate]
     9	//!                         [--write-report]
    10	//!
    11	//! Scenarios: mount-unmount (S1) | dispatch-flood (S2) | snapshot-pressure (S3)
    12	//!            | reconciler-backpressure (S4) | reentrancy (S5)
    13	//!
    14	//! See `docs/design/ffi-hardening/harness.md` §1.2 for the full CLI reference.
    15	
    16	mod allocator;
    17	mod common;
    18	mod ffi;
    19	mod gate;
    20	mod report;
    21	mod s1_mount_unmount;
    22	mod s2_dispatch_flood;
    23	mod s3_snapshot_pressure;
    24	mod s4_reconciler_backpressure;
    25	mod s5_reentrancy;
    26	
    27	use report::{now_unix_seconds, write_scenario_report, ScenarioMetrics};
    28	use std::process;
    29	use std::time::Duration;
    30	
    31	fn main() {
    32	    let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
    33	    let cli = match Cli::parse(&args[1..]) {
    34	        Ok(cli) => cli,
    35	        Err(msg) => {
    36	            eprintln!("ffi-stress: {msg}");
    37	            eprintln!("{USAGE}");
    38	            process::exit(1);
    39	        }
    40	    };
    41	
    42	    let mut overall_pass = true;
    43	
    44	    for scenario in &cli.scenarios {
    45	        let mut metrics = ScenarioMetrics::new(scenario_name(scenario));
    46	        metrics.started_at_unix = now_unix_seconds();
    47	
    48	        eprintln!("ffi-stress: running {} …", scenario_name(scenario));
    49	
    50	        match scenario {
    51	            Scenario::MountUnmount => {
    52	                let cfg = s1_mount_unmount::S1Config {
    53	                    duration: cli.duration,
    54	                    ..Default::default()
    55	                };
    56	                s1_mount_unmount::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    57	            }
    58	            Scenario::DispatchFlood => {
    59	                let cfg = s2_dispatch_flood::S2Config {
    60	                    duration: cli.duration,
    61	                    threads: cli.threads,
    62	                    ..Default::default()
    63	                };
    64	                s2_dispatch_flood::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    65	            }
    66	            Scenario::SnapshotPressure => {
    67	                let cfg = s3_snapshot_pressure::S3Config::default();
    68	                s3_snapshot_pressure::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    69	            }
    70	            Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure => {
    71	                let cfg = s4_reconciler_backpressure::S4Config {
    72	                    duration: cli.duration,
    73	                    ..Default::default()
    74	                };
    75	                s4_reconciler_backpressure::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    76	            }
    77	            Scenario::Reentrancy => {
    78	                let cfg = s5_reentrancy::S5Config {
    79	                    duration: cli.duration,
    80	                    ..Default::default()
    81	                };
    82	                s5_reentrancy::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    83	            }
    84	        }
    85	
    86	        let passed = metrics.passed;
    87	        overall_pass &= passed;
    88	
    89	        eprintln!(
    90	            "ffi-stress: {} — {} ({}/{} gates)",
    91	            scenario_name(scenario),
    92	            if passed { "PASS" } else { "FAIL" },
    93	            metrics.gates.iter().filter(|g| g.passed).count(),
    94	            metrics.gates.len()
    95	        );
    96	        for gate in &metrics.gates {
    97	            let sym = if gate.passed { "  ok" } else { "FAIL" };
    98	            eprintln!("  [{sym}] {}: {:.4} (op={:?} threshold={:.4})",
    99	                gate.name, gate.measured, gate.op, gate.threshold);
   100	        }
   101	
   102	        if cli.write_report {
   103	            if let Err(e) = write_scenario_report(&metrics) {
   104	                eprintln!("ffi-stress: failed to write report: {e}");
   105	            }
   106	        }
   107	
   108	        // Print JSON to stdout for pipeline consumption.
   109	        println!(
   110	            "{}",
   111	            serde_json::to_string_pretty(&metrics).expect("serialize")
   112	        );
   113	    }
   114	
   115	    if cli.fail_on_gate && !overall_pass {
   116	        process::exit(2);
   117	    }
   118	}
   119	
   120	// ---- CLI parsing -----------------------------------------------------------
   121	
   122	const USAGE: &str = r#"Usage: ffi-stress <scenario> [options]
   123	
   124	Scenarios:
   125	  mount-unmount            S1 — view-handle wrapper refcount churn
   126	  dispatch-flood           S2 — mpsc backpressure (10k/s, 4 threads)
   127	  snapshot-pressure        S3 — AppUpdate::FullState serialization pressure
   128	  reconciler-backpressure  S4 — 250 ms main-thread stall simulation
   129	  reentrancy               S5 — dispatch from inside reconciler callback
   130	
   131	Options:
   132	  --duration <D>           Wall-clock duration (e.g. 60s, 10m). Default: scenario-specific.
   133	  --threads <N>            Caller thread count (S2 default: 4).
   134	  --fail-on-gate           Exit 2 if any gate fails.
   135	  --write-report           Write docs/perf/m10.5/<scenario>/{metrics.json,report.md}.
   136	"#;
   137	
   138	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
   139	enum Scenario {
   140	    MountUnmount,
   141	    DispatchFlood,
   142	    SnapshotPressure,
   143	    ReconcilerBackpressure,
   144	    Reentrancy,
   145	}
   146	
   147	fn scenario_name(s: &Scenario) -> &'static str {
   148	    match s {
   149	        Scenario::MountUnmount => "S1-mount-unmount",
   150	        Scenario::DispatchFlood => "S2-dispatch-flood",
   151	        Scenario::SnapshotPressure => "S3-snapshot-pressure",
   152	        Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure => "S4-reconciler-backpressure",
   153	        Scenario::Reentrancy => "S5-reentrancy",
   154	    }
   155	}
   156	
   157	struct Cli {
   158	    scenarios: Vec<Scenario>,
   159	    duration: Duration,
   160	    threads: usize,
   161	    fail_on_gate: bool,
   162	    write_report: bool,
   163	}
   164	
   165	impl Cli {
   166	    fn parse(args: &[String]) -> Result<Cli, String> {
   167	        let mut scenarios: Vec<Scenario> = Vec::new();
   168	        let mut duration: Option<Duration> = None;
   169	        let mut threads: usize = 4;
   170	        let mut fail_on_gate = false;
   171	        let mut write_report = false;
   172	
   173	        let mut i = 0;
   174	        while i < args.len() {
   175	            match args[i].as_str() {
   176	                "--duration" => {
   177	                    i += 1;
   178	                    let s = args.get(i).ok_or("--duration requires a value")?;
   179	                    duration = Some(parse_duration(s)?);
   180	                }
   181	                "--threads" => {
   182	                    i += 1;
   183	                    let s = args.get(i).ok_or("--threads requires a value")?;
   184	                    threads = s.parse::<usize>().map_err(|_| "invalid --threads value")?;
   185	                }
   186	                "--fail-on-gate" => fail_on_gate = true,
   187	                "--write-report" => write_report = true,
   188	                s if !s.starts_with('-') => {
   189	                    let sc = parse_scenario(s)?;
   190	                    scenarios.push(sc);
   191	                }
   192	                unknown => return Err(format!("unknown argument: {unknown}")),
   193	            }
   194	            i += 1;
   195	        }
   196	
   197	        if scenarios.is_empty() {
   198	            return Err("no scenario specified".to_string());
   199	        }
   200	
   201	        // Default durations per scenario (fast mode).
   202	        let duration = duration.unwrap_or_else(|| match scenarios.first() {
   203	            Some(Scenario::MountUnmount) => Duration::from_secs(60),
   204	            Some(Scenario::DispatchFlood) => Duration::from_secs(30),
   205	            Some(Scenario::SnapshotPressure) => Duration::from_secs(30),
   206	            Some(Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure) => Duration::from_secs(60),
   207	            Some(Scenario::Reentrancy) => Duration::from_secs(30),
   208	            None => Duration::from_secs(30),
   209	        });
   210	
   211	        Ok(Cli {
   212	            scenarios,
   213	            duration,
   214	            threads,
   215	            fail_on_gate,
   216	            write_report,
   217	        })
   218	    }
   219	}
   220	
   221	fn parse_scenario(s: &str) -> Result<Scenario, String> {
   222	    match s {
   223	        "mount-unmount" | "s1" => Ok(Scenario::MountUnmount),
   224	        "dispatch-flood" | "s2" => Ok(Scenario::DispatchFlood),
   225	        "snapshot-pressure" | "s3" => Ok(Scenario::SnapshotPressure),
   226	        "reconciler-backpressure" | "s4" => Ok(Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure),
   227	        "reentrancy" | "s5" => Ok(Scenario::Reentrancy),
   228	        other => Err(format!("unknown scenario: {other}")),
   229	    }
   230	}
   231	
   232	fn parse_duration(s: &str) -> Result<Duration, String> {
   233	    if let Some(mins) = s.strip_suffix('m') {
   234	        let m: u64 = mins
   235	            .parse()
   236	            .map_err(|_| format!("invalid duration: {s}"))?;
   237	        return Ok(Duration::from_secs(m * 60));
   238	    }
   239	    if let Some(secs) = s.strip_suffix('s') {
   240	        let s: u64 = secs
   241	            .parse()
   242	            .map_err(|_| format!("invalid duration: {s}"))?;
   243	        return Ok(Duration::from_secs(s));
   244	    }
   245	    // Plain number = seconds.
   246	    s.parse::<u64>()
   247	        .map(Duration::from_secs)
   248	        .map_err(|_| format!("invalid duration: {s}"))
   249	}

//! `MailboxCache` trait, `MailboxSnapshot`, and phase-1 implementations.
//!
//! The trait is the seam between the compiler and the `nmp-nip65` crate.
//! Phase 1: `EmptyMailboxCache` + `InMemoryMailboxCache` stubs.
//! Phase 2: replaced by `nmp-nip65::InMemoryMailboxCache`.
//!
//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.1
//! Doctrine: D3 (outbox routing automatic).

use std::collections::HashMap;
use crate::planner::interest::{Pubkey, RelayUrl};

// ─── MailboxSnapshot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Minimal mailbox snapshot used by the compiler.
///
/// Phase 1: only `write_relays` and `both_relays` are consumed (Outbox
/// direction). Inbox direction (read_relays) is used for `#p` interests.
///
/// Full trait lives in `nmp-nip65::cache::MailboxCache` (later slice).
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]
pub struct MailboxSnapshot {
    pub write_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
    pub read_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
    pub both_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
}

impl MailboxSnapshot {
    /// All relays relevant for Outbox direction (write + both).
    pub fn outbox_relays(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &RelayUrl> {
        self.write_relays.iter().chain(self.both_relays.iter())
    }

    /// All relays relevant for Inbox direction (read + both).
    ///
    /// Used for `#p` interests (DMs, notifications) where we want to reach the
    /// tagged pubkey's declared read relays. `both_relays` are included because
    /// the pubkey reads from them too (NIP-65 semantics: `both` = read + write).
    pub fn inbox_relays(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &RelayUrl> {
        self.read_relays.iter().chain(self.both_relays.iter())
    }

    /// True iff the snapshot has at least one inbox relay (read or both).
    pub fn has_inbox_relays(&self) -> bool {
        !self.read_relays.is_empty() || !self.both_relays.is_empty()
    }
}

// ─── MailboxCache trait ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Minimum surface the compiler needs for mailbox lookups.
/// Phase 1 implementation: `EmptyMailboxCache` always returns `None`.
/// Phase 2 implementation: `nmp-nip65::InMemoryMailboxCache`.
pub trait MailboxCache: Send + Sync {
    fn get(&self, pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot>;
    /// Snapshot of all known entries for plan-id hashing.
    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)>;
    /// Monotonic generation counter — advances on every accepted `put`.
    fn generation(&self) -> u64;
    /// Request a background probe for a pubkey whose mailbox is unknown.
    ///
    /// Phase 1: no-op. Phase 2: the actor wires this to an `IndexerProbe`
    /// action that fetches the author's kind:10002 from the indexer set,
    /// then calls `put()` on cache arrival, triggering a recompile.
    ///
    /// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.2
    fn request_probe(&self, _pubkey: &Pubkey) {
        // Default: no-op. Implementations that own an action channel override this.
    }
}

// ─── EmptyMailboxCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Phase 1 stub: no mailbox data. All authors fall back to the indexer set.
pub struct EmptyMailboxCache;

impl MailboxCache for EmptyMailboxCache {
    fn get(&self, _pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot> {
        None
    }
    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)> {
        Vec::new()
    }
    fn generation(&self) -> u64 {
        0
    }
}

// ─── InMemoryMailboxCache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Simple in-memory mailbox cache for tests and the planner harness.
#[derive(Default)]
pub struct InMemoryMailboxCache {
    data: HashMap<Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot>,
    generation: u64,
}

impl InMemoryMailboxCache {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self::default()
    }

    pub fn put(&mut self, pubkey: Pubkey, snapshot: MailboxSnapshot) {
        self.data.insert(pubkey, snapshot);
        self.generation = self.generation.saturating_add(1);
    }
}

impl MailboxCache for InMemoryMailboxCache {
    fn get(&self, pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot> {
        self.data.get(pubkey).cloned()
    }
    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)> {
        self.data.iter().map(|(k, v)| (k.clone(), v.clone())).collect()
    }
    fn generation(&self) -> u64 {
        self.generation
    }
}
